warnings: Item 19 does not disclose a full profit/EBITDA table for franchised studios — only the affiliate studio's cost breakdown is provided; no franchisee-level net income or EBITDA cohort exists.
Special Risk #4 explicitly flags the franchisor's financial condition as calling into question its ability to provide support.

extracted.brandBackground: SPENGA is a fitness studio franchise founded in 2015 combining spin, strength, and yoga into a single 24-station class session with aromatherapy and DJ-inspired music.
extracted.conceptRationale: SPENGA operates boutique fitness studios offering structured group workout classes (spin, strength, yoga) on a membership model — a classic fitness_studio concept.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 and Item 16 require at least one trained instructor on-site at all times classes are offered, and Item 11 describes a full front-desk and instructor staffing model.

leadership.name: Roger McGreal
leadership.background: Co-founder and CEO since March 2015; previously Director of Personal Training at Cardinal Fitness and Charter Fitness.
leadership.whyItMatters: Longest-tenured leader and primary franchisor contact for all franchise sales and support.

leadership.name: Nancy Vrdolyak
leadership.background: Co-founder and EVP since January 2018; over 20 years in the fitness industry and President of Spenga Inc.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees brand strategy and holds the Spenga Inc. IP entity presidency.

leadership.name: Heather Ruff
leadership.background: Co-founder and VP of Operations since March 2015; previously Assistant Director of Personal Training at Cardinal Fitness and Charter Fitness.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads day-to-day franchise operations standards and compliance.

leadership.name: Amy Nielsen
leadership.background: Co-founder and VP of Fitness since March 2015; previously Assistant Director of Personal Training at Cardinal Fitness and Charter Fitness.
leadership.whyItMatters: Responsible for fitness programming, instructor standards, and training curriculum.

leadership.name: Joseph Vrdolyak
leadership.role: Vice President of Construction and Development
leadership.background: Co-founder and VP since December 2018; over 20 years in fitness facility development at Cardinal Fitness and Charter Fitness.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages studio build-out, site development, and construction supplier relationships.

leadership.name: Emily Hileman
leadership.background: Director of Operations since March 2022; previously General Manager of Spenga Fort Collins and Operations Support Specialist.
leadership.whyItMatters: Primary operational support contact for franchisees post-opening.

item19.notes: Item 19 is divided into 10 Parts. Part I covers the single affiliate studio's full P&L for 2025. Parts II-VIII cover operational metrics (leads, conversion, membership pricing, package revenue, no-show revenue, retail revenue, aggregator revenue) for franchised and affiliate studios. Part IX discloses NCI (gross revenue) by performance tier for 35 franchised studios open the full year meeting marketing minimums. Part X covers one new studio's presale and first-30-day NCI. No franchisee-level EBITDA or net income table is disclosed. Nine franchised studios were excluded from Part IX for not meeting minimum marketing spend.

cohorts.label: Affiliate Studio - Full P&L (2025)
cohorts.basis: Total NCI (Net Cash In) for the single affiliate-owned studio (Spenga of Mokena) for calendar year 2025; Item 19 Part I, p.60

cohorts.label: Affiliate Studio - Net After Disclosed Costs (2025)
cohorts.basis: Total NCI less payroll, COGS, estimated royalty, advertising, and rent/occupancy for the affiliate studio in 2025; Item 19 Part I, p.60

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - NCI Tier 1 Highest Performing (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual NCI for the top 8 of 35 qualifying franchised studios open the full 2025 year meeting marketing minimums; Item 19 Part IX, p.69

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - NCI Tier 2 (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual NCI for the second-tier 9 of 35 qualifying franchised studios in 2025; Item 19 Part IX, p.69

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - NCI Tier 3 (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual NCI for the third-tier 9 of 35 qualifying franchised studios in 2025; Item 19 Part IX, p.69

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - NCI Tier 4 Lowest Performing (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual NCI for the bottom 9 of 35 qualifying franchised studios in 2025; Item 19 Part IX, p.69

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - Package Sessions & Challenge Revenue (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual package session and challenge revenue for all 44 franchised studios in 2025; Item 19 Part V, p.65

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - No Show & Late Cancel Revenue (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual no-show and late-cancel fee revenue for 38 of 44 franchised studios participating in auto-billing in 2025; Item 19 Part VI, p.66

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - Retail Revenue Active Participants (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual retail revenue for 17 franchised studios actively participating in the retail program with at least $7,500 COGS in 2025; Item 19 Part VII, p.67

cohorts.label: Franchised Studios - Third-Party Aggregator Revenue (2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual third-party aggregator revenue for 37 of 44 franchised studios using multiple aggregators in 2025; Item 19 Part VIII, p.68

cohorts.label: New Franchised Studio - Presale & First 30 Days NCI (2025)
cohorts.basis: One new franchised studio conducted presales in 2025: 305 presale members and $75,469 NCI in first 30 days of operation; Item 19 Part X, p.70

lineItems.notes: Paid to franchisor upon signing Franchise Agreement; non-refundable
Traditional model; paid to landlord upon signing lease
Traditional model; low end assumes rent abatement for first 3 months
Traditional model; includes architectural, engineering, and bid assistance fees; net of tenant improvement allowance
Assumes leased/financed equipment at 10.2%-10.8% interest over 48-60 months; purchase price approximately $203,299

lineItems.notes: Decor, retail display, bathroom stands/racks, storage shelving

flatMonthlyFees.name: POS System and Payment Processing Fee

hiddenCosts.name: Instructor Training Fee (new/replacement instructors)
hiddenCosts.description: 4-day live instructor training fee of $2,500 per new instructor hired after opening; 2-day virtual option at $1,500.

hiddenCosts.name: Audition Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Per-person fee of $75 for each training audition session required.

hiddenCosts.name: Convention/Conference Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory annual convention attendance fee expected to be $1,500-$2,000 per studio.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time $10,000 fee payable to franchisor upon any transfer of the Franchise Agreement.

hiddenCosts.name: Renewal Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time $10,000 fee payable upon renewal of the Franchise Agreement.

hiddenCosts.name: Relocation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 20% of then-current Initial Franchise Fee (up to $15,000 maximum) payable upon requesting studio relocation.

hiddenCosts.name: Audit Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Actual cost of audit payable if franchisee underreports gross sales by 2% or more.

hiddenCosts.name: Interest on Late Payments
hiddenCosts.description: 1.5% per month (or highest permissible rate) on all delinquent payments outstanding more than 30 days.

hiddenCosts.name: Collateral Assignment of Lease Negotiation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated $400-$700 in attorney fees if franchisor must assist negotiating the Collateral Assignment of Lease with landlord.

hiddenCosts.name: Computer System Annual Maintenance/Upgrade
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated $1,000-$1,800 per year for optional or required maintenance, updating, and support contracts beyond the Technology Fee.

hiddenCosts.name: Pre-Sale Membership Requirement (150 memberships)
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must sell at least 150 pre-sale memberships before opening; represents a mandatory pre-opening revenue/sales obligation.

hiddenCosts.name: Rebates Received by Franchisor from Required Suppliers
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisor receives rebates of less than 1% to 10% from required suppliers including POS, equipment, AV, construction, and marketing vendors, which may inflate franchisee costs.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Financial Condition Risk
operationalRisks.description: The FDD's Special Risks section explicitly states the franchisor's financial condition calls into question its ability to provide services and support; the balance sheet shows a members' deficit of $424,117 and an SBA EIDL loan of $485,252.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p.v; Item 21 financial statements

operationalRisks.title: Shrinking System Scale
operationalRisks.description: The franchised outlet count has declined from 66 to 44 over three years (2023-2025), with net losses of 9, 8, and 5 units respectively and only 1 new opening in 2025.

operationalRisks.title: Significant Unopened Franchises
operationalRisks.description: Four franchise agreements are signed but studios not yet open as of December 31, 2025, flagged as a Special Risk that delays may cascade to new franchisees.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Royalty and Advertising Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must pay a minimum royalty of $1,000/month and local marketing of $3,000-$4,000/month regardless of sales levels; failure may result in termination.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Spouses of individual franchisees must sign the Franchise Agreement or personal guaranty, placing marital and personal assets at risk.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be mediated and litigated in Will County, Illinois, which may disadvantage out-of-state franchisees.

operationalRisks.title: Affiliate and Franchisor Competition in Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor and affiliates may operate competing fitness concepts (including Charter Fitness) within the franchisee's designated territory under different marks.

operationalRisks.title: Broad Termination Rights with Short Cure Periods
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may terminate with only 15-day cure periods for many defaults and immediately for a broad list of non-curable defaults.

operationalRisks.title: Post-Term Non-Compete (2 Years, 15-Mile Radius)
operationalRisks.description: A 2-year post-termination non-compete applies within the designated territory and a 15-mile radius of any open or developing studio.

operationalRisks.title: Required Supplier Restrictions and Franchisor Rebates
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must purchase from a long list of required suppliers; franchisor receives rebates of up to 10% from those suppliers, creating a potential cost conflict.

operationalRisks.title: Pre-Sale Membership Obligation Before Opening
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must sell at least 150 pre-sale memberships before being permitted to open, creating a significant pre-revenue sales burden.

operationalRisks.title: Renewal on Then-Current Terms
operationalRisks.description: Renewal requires signing the then-current franchise agreement, which may contain materially different terms, and paying a $10,000 renewal fee.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p.15 — leasehold improvements for combo model listed at $309,000; lease expenses for traditional model range $0-$59,898 for 3 months; no per-sqft rent rate explicitly stated in Item 7. Studios are 3,000-3,500 sq ft (combo) or 3,500-4,000 sq ft (traditional).

riskReasons: 2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisor financial distress disclosed (negative net worth) — confirm its ability to support the system against the audited statements.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $670,931 mid-point build-out, there is a $420,931 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal.

insights.conceptRationale: SPENGA operates boutique fitness studios offering structured group workout classes (spin, strength, yoga) on a membership model — a classic fitness_studio concept.

benchmark.label: Boutique fitness / studio
benchmark.dominantRisk: Member retention/churn and fixed-cost coverage — NOT cost of goods. The deal is decided by recurring membership against rent and equipment lease.
benchmark.rampNote: 9–18 months to a mature, retained member base.
considerations: COGS is trivial; the risks are member churn and covering fixed rent + equipment lease.
Ask Item 20 operators for monthly retention, average membership lifetime, and break-even member count.
Model CAC and free-trial conversion — member acquisition cost is the hidden operating line.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 15–28% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Boutique fitness / studio” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: This franchisor has negative net worth (about $424K in the red) — it owes more than it owns — though the trend isn't worsening. Understand what's funding the gap before you commit.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by CBIZ CPAs P.C.): Net income of $248K on $3.36M of revenue — roughly 0.9x spent per $1 earned. Losses are widening, not shrinking. Negative net worth (a members'/stockholders' deficit) of $424K. Total liabilities are about 1.1x total assets. Note: about 85% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.2x assets.

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Spenga Ventures LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: negative net worth — it owes more than it owns
no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
elevated unit closures (6 in the last year, about 13% of the system)
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
